speaker: VIOLA
text: What country, friends, is this?

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: This is Illyria, lady.

speaker: VIOLA
text: And what should I do in Illyria?

speaker: VIOLA
text: My brother he is in Elysium.

speaker: VIOLA
text: Perchance he is not drowned.—What think you, sailors?

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: It is perchance that you yourself were saved.

speaker: VIOLA
text: O, my poor brother! And so perchance may he be.

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: True, madam. And to comfort you with chance,

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: Assure yourself, after our ship did split,

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: When you and those poor number saved with you

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: Hung on our driving boat, I saw your brother,

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: Most provident in peril, bind himself

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: (Courage and hope both teaching him the practice)

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: To a strong mast that lived upon the sea,

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: Where, like Arion on the dolphin’s back,

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: I saw him hold acquaintance with the waves

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: So long as I could see.

speaker: VIOLA
text: For saying so, there’s gold.

speaker: VIOLA
text: Mine own escape unfoldeth to my hope,

speaker: VIOLA
text: Whereto thy speech serves for authority,

speaker: VIOLA
text: The like of him. Know’st thou this country?

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: Ay, madam, well, for I was bred and born

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: Not three hours’ travel from this very place.

speaker: VIOLA
text: Who governs here?

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: A noble duke, in nature as in name.

speaker: VIOLA
text: What is his name?

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: Orsino.

speaker: VIOLA
text: Orsino. I have heard my father name him.

speaker: VIOLA
text: He was a bachelor then.

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: And so is now, or was so very late;

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: For but a month ago I went from hence,

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: And then ’twas fresh in murmur (as, you know,

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: What great ones do the less will prattle of)

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: That he did seek the love of fair Olivia.

speaker: VIOLA
text: What’s she?

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: A virtuous maid, the daughter of a count

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: That died some twelvemonth since, then leaving her

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: In the protection of his son, her brother,

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: Who shortly also died, for whose dear love,

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: They say, she hath abjured the sight

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: And company of men.

speaker: VIOLA
text: O, that I served that lady,

speaker: VIOLA
text: And might not be delivered to the world

speaker: VIOLA
text: Till I had made mine own occasion mellow,

speaker: VIOLA
text: What my estate is.

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: That were hard to compass

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: Because she will admit no kind of suit,

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: No, not the Duke’s.

speaker: VIOLA
text: There is a fair behavior in thee, captain,

speaker: VIOLA
text: And though that nature with a beauteous wall

speaker: VIOLA
text: Doth oft close in pollution, yet of thee

speaker: VIOLA
text: I will believe thou hast a mind that suits

speaker: VIOLA
text: With this thy fair and outward character.

speaker: VIOLA
text: I prithee—and I’ll pay thee bounteously—

speaker: VIOLA
text: Conceal me what I am, and be my aid

speaker: VIOLA
text: For such disguise as haply shall become

speaker: VIOLA
text: The form of my intent. I’ll serve this duke.

speaker: VIOLA
text: Thou shalt present me as an eunuch to him.

speaker: VIOLA
text: It may be worth thy pains, for I can sing

speaker: VIOLA
text: And speak to him in many sorts of music

speaker: VIOLA
text: That will allow me very worth his service.

speaker: VIOLA
text: What else may hap, to time I will commit.

speaker: VIOLA
text: Only shape thou thy silence to my wit.

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: Be you his eunuch, and your mute I’ll be.

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: When my tongue blabs, then let mine eyes not see.

speaker: VIOLA
text: I thank thee. Lead me on.
